records.narrative: The plan is flat at 3,750 units/week for 22 of 26 weeks — that's a placeholder, not a real projection. No retail account buys on a perfectly even cadence like that. Their actual buying pace over the last 13 weeks averages 1,347/week with normal week-to-week variation. A flat plan will either leave you short when they buy heavy or overstocked when they pull back. There are 1 blank weeks (W1) where this account has historically been buying. That's roughly 962 units of volume that may be missing from the plan. Double-check whether those weeks are intentionally zeroed out or just overlooked. The plan is more than double what buying history supports in 25 weeks: W2 (5,000 planned vs. 252 expected), W3 (2,000 planned vs. 252 expected), W4 (5,000 planned vs. 252 expected) and 22 more weeks. That's 87,912 units above what we'd expect. If there's a known driver — promotion, new store rollout, distribution gain — this needs documentation. Otherwise this is a high risk of overstock.
